Study results and measurements: Relative risk 0.93 (CI 95% 0.82 -1.04) Based on data from 3,660 participants in 1 studies. (Randomized controlled) Follow up: one study with 2 cohorts (year 1 & 2). pooled for both the cohorts.
Intervention Mass testing and treatment: 351 per 1000 26 fewer per 1000 ( CI 95% 68 fewer -15 more )
Summary: MTaT does not reduce the prevalence of malaria at 2 months.

Study results and measurements: Rate ratio 0.95 (CI 95% 0.87 -1.04) Based on data from 857 participants in 1 studies. (Randomized controlled)
Intervention Mass testing and treatment: 2,214 per 1000 117 fewer per 1000 ( CI 95% 303 fewer -93 more )
Summary: MTaT does not reduce incidence of malaria infection between 0-12 months.

Study results and measurements: Relative risk 0.81 (CI 95% 0.7 -0.95) Based on data from 332,454 participants in 2 studies. (Randomized controlled)
Intervention Mass testing and treatment: 189 per 1000 44 fewer per 1000 ( CI 95% 70 fewer -12 fewer )
Summary: MTaT reduces the incidence of clinical malaria between 0-12 months.

Study results and measurements: Relative risk 1.27 (CI 95% 0.51 -3.14) Based on data from 2,349 participants in 2 studies. (Randomized controlled) Follow up: One study has two intervention arms. Both intervention arms are pooled with another study and compared with the control. Control arm is inflated in value because it's the same comparison group for the two different intervention arm in one study.
Intervention Mass testing and treatment: 5 per 1000 1 more per 1000 ( CI 95% 2 fewer -11 more )
Summary: MTaT likely results in little to no difference in the incidence of malaria infection between 6-12 months (outcome measured only in children).
